mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0% reflects excess slack in the labor market, depressing wage growth and consumer spending, which causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index raise real discount rates and production costs, lowering equity valuation multiples and earnings expectations to generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL produces positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months or achieves a Rank IC greater than 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals an impending growth contraction, triggering de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% signals weakening manufacturing activity and negative operating leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops exceeding 0.5% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm broad-based household demand strength, lifting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in HOUST and RSAFS coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1.0% reflects an emergency monetary stance that coincides with crisis-level risk aversion and depressed earnings visibility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, 5-day SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer spending momentum, driving top-line revenue beats and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth months produce negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.
